info.title: Mercury Web Parser API
info.description: Mercury Parser extracts meaningful content from any URL. It returns article content, titles, authors, published dates, excerpts, lead images, and more. Originally a hosted API by Postlight, Mercury is now an open-source library. This spec documents the hosted API interface that was available at mercury.postlight.com.

servers.description: Mercury Web Parser API (legacy hosted service)

tags.name: Parser
tags.description: Web content parsing and extraction endpoints

get.description: Extract the main content, metadata, and structured information from the given URL. Mercury uses a combination of generic content extraction and site-specific selectors to identify and extract the most relevant content from a page.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL of the web page to parse and extract content from

parameters.name: contentType
parameters.description: The desired format for the extracted content. Defaults to HTML.

parameters.name: headers
parameters.description: Custom HTTP headers to send when fetching the URL (JSON-encoded object). Useful for passing cookies or custom user agents.

200.description: Successfully parsed the web page content

400.description: Bad request - invalid URL or missing required parameters

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing API key

404.description: The URL could not be fetched or content could not be extracted

429.description: Too many requests - rate limit exceeded

post.summary: Parse a web page with pre-fetched HTML
post.description: Parse content from pre-fetched HTML. Useful when you have already fetched the page content and want Mercury to extract the article from it without fetching the URL again.

200.description: Successfully parsed the provided HTML content

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing API key

ApiKeyAuth.name: x-api-key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Obtain from the Mercury dashboard.

ParseResult.description: The result of parsing a web page

title.description: The title of the article or page

author.description: The author of the article, if found

date_published.description: The publication date of the article in ISO 8601 format

lead_image_url.description: URL of the main/lead image for the article

content.description: The extracted article content in the requested format (HTML, Markdown, or plain text)

excerpt.description: A short excerpt or summary of the article content

word_count.description: The number of words in the extracted content

direction.description: The text direction of the content

total_pages.description: Total number of pages if the article is paginated

rendered_pages.description: The number of pages that were rendered/extracted

next_page_url.description: URL of the next page for paginated articles

url.description: The original URL that was parsed

domain.description: The domain name of the parsed URL

url.description: The original URL of the content (used for resolving relative links and site-specific parsing)

html.description: The pre-fetched HTML content to parse

contentType.description: The desired format for the extracted content
